persona_panel.selection_rationale: Sampled 3 personas from panel 'mainstream' using preset 'frontline_persona_picker'. Eligible candidates moved from 3 to 3 after filters (no extra filters). Most common occupations in the final sample: program manager (2), finance manager (1).

selected_personas.name: Devon Chu
selected_personas.trust_threshold: needs proof from concrete workflows, not just positioning
selected_personas.proof_threshold: ['specific examples from similar users', 'a short trial workflow', 'evidence that the workflow can survive internal review and comparison', 'clear before-and-after value', 'low-friction onboarding']

human_difference_axes.control_preference: Hybrid; wants room to decide but appreciates clear defaults.
human_difference_axes.trust_style: Evidence-first with cautious benefit of the doubt for credible proof.
human_difference_axes.complexity_tolerance: Moderate; will engage when detail clearly helps the decision.
human_difference_axes.decision_tempo: Measured; gathers enough signal before acting.
human_difference_axes.financial_attention_cadence: Periodic with event-driven spikes when consequences rise.
human_difference_axes.relationship_to_money: Money is a practical buffer and progress marker.
human_difference_axes.risk_orientation: Open to measured risk when the downside is understandable.
human_difference_axes.need_for_explanation: High enough to want plain-language framing and concrete examples.
human_difference_axes.life_load: Moderate; daily life leaves limited room for unnecessary friction.
human_difference_axes.fragmentation_reality: Some information and decisions live across more than one place.
human_difference_axes.guidance_preference: Hybrid self-serve plus optional expert clarification.
human_difference_axes.reflection_style: Explains decisions through recent examples and trade-offs.

decision_policy.adoption_style: waits for evidence and edge-case answers before changing habits
trust_requirements: settings that prove the respectful copy is operationally real
rejection_triggers: identity assumptions hidden inside ordinary onboarding
proof_requirements: a core flow that works with minimal disclosure
clear control over what becomes visible and when
